question: Does the generated bottle retain the rectangular silhouette with rounded shoulders from the subject reference (Image 1), explicitly overriding the round shape shown in the structure reference (Image 3)?
Does the bottle feature a cylindrical gold cap matching the subject reference?
Is the liquid inside the bottle depicted as a pale pink color?
Does the bottle surface appear matte and opaque like clay, rather than transparent and glossy like glass?
Are there visible surface imperfections, such as fingerprints, slight unevenness, or hand-molding marks, consistent with the claymation style reference (Image 2)?
Is the gold cap rendered with a soft, satin, or matte finish typical of clay animation, rather than a highly reflective photorealistic metal finish?
Is the perfume bottle positioned in the right-center of the frame with an oval mirror standing directly behind it?
Are the makeup brushes and tray visible in the bottom-left foreground, matching the layout of the structure reference?
Does the mirror reflection accurately show the back of the bottle with a liquid level consistent with the main object?
Is the label text either absent or rendered as illegible abstract markings, avoiding distinct garbled characters?
Do the objects cast realistic contact shadows on the surface, preventing them from appearing to float?
Is the background a neutral surface (e.g., vanity table) consistent with the structure reference, avoiding the forest environment from the style reference?
